summary: Linked Jigglypuff/Purin side special `ftPr_SpecialS.c` as a 100% matching C object, worth 1.06 KB in the PR report. The work isolated the still-nonmatching neutral-special region into a new `ftPr_SpecialN.c`/`.h` pair and renamed the remaining asm file to `ftPr_SpecialN.s`, while switching `ftPr_SpecialS.c` from asm/nonmatching to source/matching in the build configuration.
what_changed: `configure.py` changed `melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialS.c` from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)` and added `ftPr_SpecialN.c` as the new nonmatching object.
`obj_files.mk` replaced the side-special asm object with `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialS.c.o` and added the remaining asm as `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN.s.o`.
The old `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialS.s` was renamed to `ftPr_SpecialN.s` and trimmed so the side-special functions no longer lived in asm.

`ftPr_SpecialS.c` kept the side-special functions `ftPr_SpecialS_Enter`, `ftPr_SpecialAirS_Enter`, `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, `Coll`, `ftPr_SpecialS_8013D590`, and `ftPr_SpecialS_8013D5F0`, and implemented `ftPr_SpecialAirS_Phys` in C.
The neutral-special functions and placeholders previously mixed into `ftPr_SpecialS.c` were moved into new `ftPr_SpecialN.c` and declared in new `ftPr_SpecialN.h`.
`ftPr_SpecialS.h` was reduced to side-special declarations only; the neutral-special declarations were removed from it.
`ftPr_Init.c` and `ftdata.c` now include `ftPr_SpecialN.h` in addition to `ftPr_SpecialS.h`.

`types.h` refined Purin-specific fighter vars and motion vars, including changing `x2230` to `Vec3`, expanding `specialn` motion vars, adding `specialn_vel`, and asserting `sizeof(ftPurinAttributes) == 0x100`.
`config/GALE01/splits.txt`, `symbols.txt`, and `scratches.txt` were updated to reflect the linked side-special source split and remaining neutral-special asm/source split.
smart_moves: Separated `SpecialS` from the adjacent `SpecialN` code instead of trying to link a mixed file; this let a small fully matching side-special unit be promoted while preserving the larger neutral-special work as nonmatching/asm.
Moved many declarations out of `ftPr_SpecialS.h` into the new `ftPr_SpecialN.h`, making headers align with actual file ownership and reducing accidental coupling.
Encoded the aerial side-special angle calculation as a local inline helper `calcAngleRadians`, matching the clamp/sign behavior while making the trigonometry readable.
Used literal `0`, `1`, and `deg_to_rad`/small force-float-order helpers rather than only extern float symbols, indicating the match depended on float constant ordering.
Generalized ad hoc `u64` stack padding patterns into named `FORCE_PAD_STACK_*` macros and reused them in `ftMs_SpecialN.c`, making a matching tactic reusable.
Added explicit Purin attribute and motion-var fields instead of leaving a large filler blob, improving future decompilation readability around Rollout/Pound state.
decomp_lessons: If a source file contains multiple move groups with different match status, split the remaining asm/nonmatching range into a separate file and promote only the matched unit.
Header ownership matters: declarations for neutral-special functions should live in `ftPr_SpecialN.h`, while `ftPr_SpecialS.h` should only expose side-special entrypoints/callbacks.
Small float constants can affect sdata2 ordering; sometimes a match needs explicit float-order forcing or replacing extern float loads with literals where the compiler emits the expected constants.
Use structured fighter vars and data attributes once offsets are understood; this makes motion-state code much easier to express and reduces offset magic.
For stack-shape matching, prefer reusable macros such as `PAD_STACK` and `FORCE_PAD_STACK_*` over anonymous local dummy arrays or repeated local `u64` blocks.
Build metadata must be updated consistently across `configure.py`, `obj_files.mk`, splits, symbols, and scratches when replacing an asm object with a matching C object.
naming_conventions: Move files follow `ftPr_SpecialS` for side special and `ftPr_SpecialN` for neutral special.
Ground/aerial pairs use `SpecialS` and `SpecialAirS` naming, e.g. `ftPr_SpecialS_Enter` and `ftPr_SpecialAirS_Enter`.
Standard fighter callbacks use suffixes `Enter`, `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, and `Coll`.
Unknown helper functions retain address-suffixed names such as `ftPr_SpecialS_8013D590`, `ftPr_SpecialS_8013D5F0`, and `ftPr_SpecialS_8013D658`.
Purin-specific data was grouped under `fp->fv.pr` and `fp->mv.pr.specialn`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the asm object for `ftPr_SpecialS` with a matching C object and renamed the remaining asm file to `ftPr_SpecialN.s`.
Used `PAD_STACK(4 * 6)` in `ftPr_SpecialAirS_Phys` to match stack layout.
Introduced `FORCE_PAD_STACK_8`, `FORCE_PAD_STACK_16`, and `FORCE_PAD_STACK_32` for dummy `u64` stack slots required by matching code.
Added `forceFloatOrder0`, `forceFloatOrder1`, and `forceFloatOrder2` static helpers in `ftPr_SpecialS.c` with comments marking them as float-order hacks.
Expressed aerial side-special velocity with `sinf` and `cosf` over `deg_to_rad * degrees`, matching the original angle calculation.
Kept motion flags as exact constants such as `0x0C4C508AU` where named flag coverage was not yet available.
Converted string data in asm from raw `.4byte` values to `.string` plus explicit hidden gap objects for alignment/padding.
follow_up_queries: How was the remaining `ftPr_SpecialN.c` later linked after this split?
Can `ftPr_SpecialS_8013D590` and `ftPr_SpecialS_8013D5F0` receive semantic names for ground/air transition callbacks?
Can the hard-coded motion flag constants such as `0x0C4C508A` be replaced by named `MotionFlags` bits?
Are the float-order helper functions still needed after later sdata2 cleanup?
Can Purin attribute fields `xDC`, `xE0`, `xE4`, `xF0`, and `xF4` be named from side-special behavior?
